purpose: Integrate Hermes Agent as an external, governed Ananta worker backend next to native Ananta Worker, OpenCode and other adapters, without allowing Hermes to become a control plane or bypass Hub policy.
goal: Provide a safe HermesWorkerAdapter that can execute plan_only, review, research, summarize and patch_propose style work through Ananta ExecutionEnvelope, ContextEnvelope, ToolRouter, Approval, Artifact and TraceBundle contracts.

small_model_guidance.intent: This todo is written so weaker/smaller models can implement it step by step without knowing Hermes internals.
rules: Do not skip contract and negative-test tasks.
If uncertain, implement the safer behavior: deny, no mutation, no cloud context, no direct file writes.
Prefer narrow working code with tests over broad clever automation.
Every adapter output must become an Ananta artifact before it is trusted.

non_goals: Do not let Hermes create, reprioritize, approve or execute Ananta goals/tasks directly.
Do not let Hermes call shell, browser, MCP or file mutation tools on Ananta's behalf in phase 1.
Do not let Hermes receive full repositories or secrets by default.
Do not use Hermes memory as Ananta project truth.
Do not accept natural-language success claims from Hermes as execution proof.
Do not implement patch_apply through Hermes.
Do not add a YOLO or unrestricted mode.
architecture_guardrails: Ananta Hub remains the only control plane.
Hermes is an external_agent_worker adapter only.
Hermes receives a bounded task prompt and bounded context, not authority.
Hermes output is untrusted until parsed, validated and converted into Ananta artifacts.
All Hermes calls must include ExecutionEnvelope-derived policy and trace metadata.
All Hermes calls must respect model_policy.cloud_allowed and context sensitivity.
No Hermes result may directly mutate the workspace, task state, memory, schedules or tools.
If parsing, policy, approval, context, endpoint or provider validation fails, return explicit denied/degraded/parse_error WorkerResult.

milestones.title: Hermes API client and health checks

tasks.title: Write ADR for Hermes as external governed worker
tasks.objective: Document that Hermes is an adapter-backed worker target, not an Ananta control plane.
implementation_notes: Create docs/architecture/hermes-worker-adapter.md.
Use simple language and diagrams.
Keep the first version short enough for small models to understand.
acceptance_criteria: Document states that Ananta Hub owns goals, tasks, policy, approval, routing, context, audit and final state changes.
Document states that Hermes may only return proposals, reviews, summaries, research notes and patch proposals in phase 1.
Document explicitly forbids Hermes patch_apply, command_execute, direct file_write, direct task mutation, direct memory_write and direct cron creation.
Document includes a simple flow: Hub -> HermesAdapter -> Hermes API -> Parsed Ananta Artifact -> Hub review/approval.
Document includes a 'wrong implementation' section with at least five forbidden shortcuts.
test_requirements: No runtime test required.
Documentation path is referenced from README or developer docs.
anti_patterns: Do not describe Hermes as a scheduler or planner owner.
Do not let docs imply that Hermes can run shell commands through Ananta in phase 1.

tasks.title: Define Hermes worker capability profile
tasks.objective: Create the default allowed and denied capability profile for Hermes.
implementation_notes: Prefer config/data object first, code second.
Make denied capabilities explicit, not implicit.
acceptance_criteria: Profile allows only planning, summarize, research_limited, code_review and patch_propose by default.
Profile denies patch_apply, shell_execute, file_write, service_mutation, config_mutation, memory_write, cron_schedule, unrestricted_network and mcp_call by default.
Profile contains risk_class='medium' or stricter by default.
Profile declares requires_structured_output=true.
Profile declares max_context_policy='bounded' and default_cloud_allowed=false unless explicitly configured by Hub policy.
Unknown Hermes capability is denied.
test_requirements: Unit test verifies allowed capabilities.
Unit test verifies denied capabilities.
Unit test verifies unknown capability is denied.
anti_patterns: Do not use an allow-all list.
Do not let Hermes inherit OpenCode or native worker capabilities automatically.

tasks.title: Add Hermes adapter configuration schema
tasks.objective: Add typed configuration for the Hermes endpoint and safe defaults.
implementation_notes: Configuration should be readable from existing Ananta config system.
Use explicit names; avoid magic env-only behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports enabled, base_url, api_key_env, default_model, timeout_seconds, max_retries, allowed_task_kinds, blocked_task_kinds, cloud_allowed, max_context_chars and strict_json_required.
Default enabled=false unless product decision says otherwise.
Default base_url is empty or local-only placeholder, not a public cloud URL.
Default blocked_task_kinds includes patch_apply and command_execute.
Config validation rejects invalid URL, negative timeout, empty model when enabled, and contradictory allowed/blocked task kind entries.
Config can be serialized without exposing API key value.
test_requirements: Unit tests for valid and invalid config.
Snapshot or assertion proving API key value is not emitted by diagnostics.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode a user's API key.
Do not silently enable Hermes just because env vars are present.

tasks.title: Define Hermes integration phases
tasks.objective: Prevent big-bang implementation by defining safe rollout phases.
implementation_notes: Add phases to docs and config comments.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 includes plan_only, review, summarize and patch_propose only.
Phase 2 may add limited research with URL/network policy.
Phase 3 may add optional delegated sandbox work only after separate approval and tests.
No phase includes direct patch_apply by Hermes unless a future ADR explicitly replaces this rule.
Tasks and tests reference phase 1 as the initial implementation target.
test_requirements: No runtime test required.
Config or docs expose phase 1 defaults.
anti_patterns: Do not implement all Hermes features at once.

tasks.title: Create HermesAdapter interface implementation skeleton
tasks.objective: Create a small, testable adapter class before adding complex behavior.
implementation_notes: Suggested path: agent/services/external_agent_adapters/hermes_adapter.py or existing adapter package.
Keep constructor dependency-injected for config, client and clock where practical.
acceptance_criteria: HermesAdapter class exists with id='hermes'.
Adapter exposes health(), propose(envelope), review(envelope), summarize(envelope), patch_propose(envelope) or equivalent typed methods.
Every public execution method requires ExecutionEnvelope or existing Ananta task-scoped equivalent.
All methods initially return explicit not_implemented/degraded WorkerResult until implemented, not raw exceptions.
Adapter has no direct shell execution or direct file mutation code.
test_requirements: Unit test imports adapter.
Unit test calls health with disabled config.
Unit test verifies direct mutation methods do not exist.
anti_patterns: Do not add Hermes logic inside TaskScopedExecutionService as a giant if block.
Do not call requests/curl directly from many places.

tasks.title: Register Hermes in BridgeAdapterRegistry
tasks.objective: Make Hermes discoverable through the existing adapter registry, not hardcoded routing.
implementation_notes: Use existing BridgeAdapterRegistry style if available.
If registry shape is insufficient, add the smallest extension needed.
acceptance_criteria: Hermes appears as adapter id 'hermes'.
Registry entry exposes kind='external_agent_worker'.
Registry entry exposes allowed and denied capability classes from Hermes profile.
Registry entry exposes health status without making a model call when disabled.
Registry lookup for Hermes returns deterministic metadata for ToolRouter/diagnostics.
test_requirements: Unit test verifies Hermes registry entry exists when feature enabled.
Unit test verifies Hermes is absent or disabled when feature disabled.
Unit test verifies denied capabilities are visible in metadata.
anti_patterns: Do not bypass registry by manually instantiating HermesAdapter from route code.

tasks.title: Register Hermes capabilities in CapabilityRegistry
tasks.objective: Ensure policy can reason about Hermes before routing tasks to it.
implementation_notes: Use existing CapabilityRegistry if present.
Make blocked capabilities explicit for audit/debug views.
acceptance_criteria: CapabilityRegistry can answer whether Hermes supports a requested capability.
CapabilityRegistry denies shell_execute, patch_apply, file_write, memory_write and cron_schedule for Hermes by default.
CapabilityRegistry includes Hermes risk_class and requires_structured_output metadata.
Unknown task kind or capability produces deny/default-deny outcome.
Routing can use this metadata without importing HermesAdapter implementation directly.
test_requirements: Unit tests verify allowed/denied capability decisions.
Negative test verifies unknown capability denied.
anti_patterns: Do not let the adapter self-assert capabilities at runtime without registry policy.

tasks.title: Add ToolRouter support for Hermes candidate selection
tasks.objective: Allow Hub routing to select Hermes for safe task kinds only.
implementation_notes: Selection should be explainable.
Keep existing native/OpenCode routing behavior intact.
acceptance_criteria: ToolRouter may select Hermes for plan_only, review, summarize, patch_propose and optionally research_limited.
ToolRouter never selects Hermes for patch_apply, command_execute, service_mutation or config_mutation.
Routing decision includes selected_reason and alternatives with rejection reasons.
If Hermes is unavailable, router chooses another eligible backend or returns degraded/no_backend_available.
Routing respects governance mode; strict mode is at least as restrictive as safe/balanced.
test_requirements: Routing unit tests for each allowed task kind.
Routing unit tests for denied task kinds.
Availability fallback test.
anti_patterns: Do not pick Hermes because it is configured if task kind is unsafe.

tasks.title: Add feature flag for Hermes worker adapter
tasks.objective: Allow safe rollout and quick rollback.
implementation_notes: Use existing config style.
acceptance_criteria: Feature flag controls whether Hermes appears in registry and routing.
Disabled flag prevents Hermes calls even if endpoint and API key exist.
Diagnostics show disabled_by_feature_flag where applicable.
Tests verify no Hermes request occurs when flag disabled.
test_requirements: Feature flag unit tests.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on missing API key to disable Hermes.

tasks.title: Implement Hermes HTTP client wrapper
tasks.objective: Create one safe HTTP client path for Hermes API calls.
implementation_notes: Client should support OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions first.
Avoid streaming in first slice unless already easy.
acceptance_criteria: Client accepts base_url, api_key, timeout_seconds and model from config/envelope.
Client supports chat_completions request with system/user messages and max_tokens/temperature where configured.
Client maps HTTP timeout, connection error, 401/403, 404, 429 and 5xx into structured error codes.
Client redacts Authorization headers and keys from logs/errors.
Client has no business policy logic; policy stays in adapter/router layers.
test_requirements: Mock HTTP tests for success, timeout, unauthorized, rate limit and server error.
Redaction test for headers and API key.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter raw HTTP calls throughout adapter methods.
Do not log raw request headers.

tasks.title: Implement Hermes health check
tasks.objective: Detect configured/available/degraded states before routing.
implementation_notes: Use cheap endpoint where possible, e.g. models endpoint if available.
acceptance_criteria: health() returns disabled, ready, degraded, unauthorized, unavailable or misconfigured.
Health check does not perform an expensive model generation.
Health result includes sanitized base_url, selected model and reason_code.
Health result never includes API key.
Unavailable Hermes does not crash task routing.
test_requirements: Mock health tests for disabled, ready, unauthorized and unavailable.
Diagnostics redaction test.
anti_patterns: Do not use a real expensive prompt as health check.

tasks.title: Add Hermes model selection rules
tasks.objective: Choose model only from Hub/config policy, not from Hermes or prompt text.
implementation_notes: Start with one default model.
Add task-specific override only if already supported by config.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter uses model from ExecutionEnvelope when present and allowed.
Adapter falls back to configured default_model only when envelope does not specify one.
Adapter rejects models blocked by model_policy.
Adapter does not let Hermes auto-switch model for Ananta tasks.
Trace records requested_model and effective_model.
test_requirements: Unit test for envelope model override.
Unit test for blocked model.
Unit test for default model fallback.
anti_patterns: Do not parse model name from user prompt.

tasks.title: Add Hermes call timeout and retry policy
tasks.objective: Make Hermes calls bounded and predictable.
implementation_notes: Default retry count should be low.
Do not retry unsafe side-effecting calls; phase 1 has no side effects anyway.
acceptance_criteria: Timeout defaults to a bounded value from config.
Retry count is bounded and configurable.
Retries happen only for transient errors such as timeout/429/5xx according to policy.
Final WorkerResult distinguishes timeout, rate_limited and remote_error.
Trace records retry_count and total_duration_ms.
test_requirements: Mock tests for retry and no-retry cases.
Test verifies total wait is bounded.
anti_patterns: Do not implement unbounded retries.

tasks.title: Build Hermes system prompt template for governed worker mode
tasks.objective: Give Hermes clear task instructions while enforcement remains in code.
implementation_notes: Keep prompt short, strict and repeated in tests.
Prompt is not a security boundary; code must enforce too.
acceptance_criteria: System prompt says Hermes is an external Ananta worker and cannot claim side effects.
System prompt says output must be strict JSON matching requested schema.
System prompt says no shell execution, file write, patch apply, memory write, cron creation or direct task mutation is allowed.
System prompt includes allowed_mode and denied_operations from ExecutionEnvelope.
Prompt contains no secrets or full internal policy dump.
test_requirements: Snapshot test for prompt with representative envelope.
Test verifies denied operations appear in prompt.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on prompt as the only security control.
Do not include raw secrets in prompt.

tasks.title: Build bounded ContextEnvelope to Hermes prompt conversion
tasks.objective: Send only allowed, bounded, sanitized context to Hermes.
implementation_notes: Use existing ContextManager where possible.
Never send whole repo in first implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Converter accepts only context blocks already approved by Hub/ContextManager.
Converter enforces max_context_chars or token-equivalent budget.
Converter includes provenance labels for each context block.
Converter excludes secret/sensitive blocks unless policy explicitly allows them.
Converter records included, skipped and truncated context items with reason codes.
If context is missing or too sensitive, adapter returns denied/degraded rather than asking Hermes to guess.
test_requirements: Unit test for budget truncation.
Unit test for sensitive block exclusion.
Unit test for missing required context.
anti_patterns: Do not send full repository dumps.
Do not let Hermes request additional files directly.

tasks.title: Define Hermes structured output schemas
tasks.objective: Make Hermes output machine-parseable and safe to validate.
implementation_notes: Keep schemas simple for smaller models.
Use one common wrapper with task-specific payload.
acceptance_criteria: Common schema includes status, artifact_type, summary, findings, risks, suggested_tests, confidence, requires_approval_for_apply and no_side_effects_claimed.
Patch proposal schema includes patch_unified_diff or patch_description and touched_files.
Review schema includes findings with severity, file/path if known, rationale and recommendation.
Research schema includes claims, sources if provided and uncertainty.
Invalid or missing required fields cause parse_error WorkerResult.
test_requirements: Schema validation tests for valid outputs.
Negative tests for free text, missing artifact_type and unsafe success claim.
anti_patterns: Do not accept arbitrary Markdown as successful structured output.

tasks.title: Implement Hermes output parser
tasks.objective: Parse and validate Hermes response before converting to artifacts.
implementation_notes: Allow fenced JSON recovery only if safe and deterministic.
Do not ask a second model to repair unsafe output in first slice.
acceptance_criteria: Parser accepts strict JSON object matching schemas.
Parser may strip a single JSON code fence if content is otherwise valid.
Parser rejects free text mixed with multiple contradictory JSON objects.
Parser rejects output that claims files were modified, commands were executed or approvals were granted by Hermes.
Parser returns structured parse_error with sanitized raw snippet when invalid.
test_requirements: Parser unit tests for strict JSON, fenced JSON, free text, malformed JSON and unsafe side-effect claims.
Test verifies raw snippet is bounded and redacted.
anti_patterns: Do not silently treat unparsable output as success.

tasks.title: Add small-model fallback prompt for parse errors
tasks.objective: Improve reliability with weaker models without weakening safety.
implementation_notes: One retry max.
Retry asks for JSON only, not new reasoning.
acceptance_criteria: When strict_json_required=true and first response is malformed, adapter may perform one JSON-format retry if policy allows.
Retry prompt includes previous sanitized response and exact schema.
Retry cannot add capabilities or change task mode.
If retry fails, WorkerResult is parse_error.
Trace records parse_retry_used=true when used.
test_requirements: Mock test where retry repairs JSON.
Mock test where retry still fails and returns parse_error.
Test verifies no second retry.
anti_patterns: Do not keep retrying until model complies.

tasks.title: Implement plan_only mode through Hermes
tasks.objective: First safe end-to-end Hermes mode: generate a plan artifact only.
implementation_notes: This is the first functional mode to implement.
acceptance_criteria: plan_only call builds bounded prompt from ExecutionEnvelope and ContextEnvelope.
Hermes response is parsed into PlanArtifact.
PlanArtifact contains steps, assumptions, risks and validation suggestions.
No file, shell, task state or memory mutation occurs.
WorkerResult status is success only when PlanArtifact validates.
test_requirements: End-to-end mock Hermes plan_only test.
Negative test ensures no mutation capability is required.
anti_patterns: Do not execute commands from the generated plan.

tasks.title: Implement review mode through Hermes
tasks.objective: Use Hermes for code/change review without mutation.
implementation_notes: Input should normally be patch/context snippets, not full repo.
acceptance_criteria: review call requires code_review or review capability.
Review output is converted into ReviewArtifact or VerificationArtifact.
Findings include severity, rationale and recommendation.
Review mode cannot apply fixes.
Review mode clearly marks uncertainty when context is incomplete.
test_requirements: Mock review success test.
Test verifies patch_apply is not called.
Test verifies incomplete context warning is preserved.
anti_patterns: Do not turn review suggestions into applied changes automatically.

tasks.title: Implement summarize mode through Hermes
tasks.objective: Use Hermes for bounded summarization of artifacts, traces or context.
implementation_notes: Useful for dashboards and operator summaries.
acceptance_criteria: summarize call requires summarize capability.
Input is budget-limited and provenance-labeled.
Output is SummaryArtifact with source refs and uncertainty notes.
Summary does not become authoritative replacement for original artifacts.
Sensitive content is redacted according to context policy.
test_requirements: Mock summarize test.
Sensitive-content redaction test.
anti_patterns: Do not discard source references after summary generation.

tasks.title: Implement patch_propose mode through Hermes
tasks.objective: Allow Hermes to propose patches but never apply them.
implementation_notes: This should output PatchArtifact only.
acceptance_criteria: patch_propose requires patch_propose capability.
Output is PatchArtifact with unified diff or structured patch description.
PatchArtifact includes touched_files, rationale, risks and suggested_tests.
PatchArtifact marks requires_approval_for_apply=true.
Adapter does not write patch to workspace except as artifact storage through Ananta artifact path.
patch_apply remains routed to native Ananta approval-gated path, not Hermes.
test_requirements: Mock patch_propose success test.
Negative test rejects output claiming patch already applied.
Negative test verifies no workspace file changed.
anti_patterns: Do not run git apply from Hermes adapter.

tasks.title: Implement research_limited mode through Hermes
tasks.objective: Allow limited research only when network/context policy permits it.
implementation_notes: Phase 1 may skip this if Hermes itself would browse externally.
Prefer passing Ananta-collected research context to Hermes instead of letting Hermes browse.
acceptance_criteria: research_limited requires explicit research_limited capability.
Default implementation does not allow Hermes browser/web tools; it summarizes Ananta-provided research context.
If external web access is later enabled, it must be controlled by separate network policy and domain allow/deny rules.
ResearchArtifact includes claims, sources, uncertainty and timestamp.
Adapter refuses research task when it would require unrestricted network access.
test_requirements: Test verifies research without capability is denied.
Test verifies unrestricted network request is denied.
Mock success test using pre-collected research context.
anti_patterns: Do not let Hermes browse internal or arbitrary URLs by default.

tasks.title: Map Hermes parsed output to Ananta artifacts
tasks.objective: Convert every successful Hermes output into first-class Ananta artifacts.
implementation_notes: No successful WorkerResult without artifact.
acceptance_criteria: plan_only produces PlanArtifact.
review produces ReviewArtifact or VerificationArtifact.
summarize produces SummaryArtifact.
patch_propose produces PatchArtifact.
research_limited produces ResearchArtifact.
Artifacts include source='hermes', adapter_version, model, content_hash and context_hash.
Artifact creation failure returns failed/degraded WorkerResult, not success.
test_requirements: Unit tests for each artifact mapping.
Negative test for artifact storage failure.
anti_patterns: Do not store only raw Hermes response text as final output.

tasks.title: Add Hermes TraceBundle fields
tasks.objective: Make Hermes executions debuggable and auditable.
implementation_notes: Reuse existing trace structure where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Trace records adapter_id='hermes', base_url_sanitized, model, task_mode, capability_hash, context_hash, prompt_hash, response_hash, duration_ms, retry_count and parse_retry_used.
Trace records routing decision id if available.
Trace records denial/degraded reason codes.
Trace does not include raw API key, Authorization header or unredacted secret content.
Trace exists for success, denied, parse_error, timeout and remote_error.
test_requirements: Trace snapshot tests for success and failure.
Redaction tests.
anti_patterns: Do not log full prompts by default.

tasks.title: Return WorkerResult for Hermes calls
tasks.objective: Normalize Hermes outcomes into existing Ananta worker response shape.
implementation_notes: This is needed for integration with task flow.
acceptance_criteria: HermesAdapter returns WorkerResult or existing compatible Ananta response object.
WorkerResult includes status, summary, artifacts, trace_bundle, warnings, degraded_state and reason_code where applicable.
Status values distinguish success, denied, needs_approval, parse_error, timeout, remote_error, failed and degraded.
No successful WorkerResult is returned without at least one artifact for non-trivial modes.
All errors are sanitized for user/operator display.
test_requirements: WorkerResult tests for all major status values.
Integration mock test through adapter method.
anti_patterns: Do not return raw Hermes HTTP response directly to task endpoints.

tasks.title: Emit audit events for Hermes execution
tasks.objective: Make Hermes use visible in Ananta audit history.
implementation_notes: Use existing audit mechanism if available.
acceptance_criteria: Audit event emitted when Hermes is selected by routing.
Audit event emitted before remote Hermes call with sanitized metadata.
Audit event emitted after call with status and artifact ids.
Audit event emitted for denial, parse_error and remote_error.
Audit event includes correlation id but no secrets.
test_requirements: Audit event unit/integration test for success and denial.
anti_patterns: Do not make remote LLM calls invisible to audit.

tasks.title: Add secret redaction for Hermes prompts and responses
tasks.objective: Prevent secrets from reaching Hermes or being persisted from Hermes output.
implementation_notes: Reuse existing redaction utilities if available.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt/context builder redacts common API keys, tokens, private keys, Authorization headers, .env values and database passwords.
Response parser redacts secrets before logging parse errors or raw snippets.
Artifacts do not store unredacted detected secrets unless explicitly classified and allowed by policy.
Redaction preserves enough structure for debugging.
Tests cover at least API key, Bearer token, private key header, DB URL password and .env assignment.
test_requirements: Secret redaction regression tests.
anti_patterns: Do not assume context manager already removed all secrets.

tasks.title: Add cloud_allowed enforcement for Hermes
tasks.objective: Prevent accidental cloud leakage when Hermes endpoint is remote.
implementation_notes: Treat non-localhost/non-private endpoints as cloud unless explicitly configured otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter classifies Hermes endpoint as local, private_network or cloud.
If endpoint is cloud and model_policy.cloud_allowed=false, call is denied before prompt construction.
If context sensitivity forbids cloud, call is denied even when endpoint is configured.
Denial reason code is cloud_blocked or sensitivity_blocked.
Trace records endpoint classification.
test_requirements: Unit tests for localhost/private/cloud endpoint classification.
Negative test cloud endpoint with cloud_allowed=false.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every configured endpoint as safe/local.

tasks.title: Block Hermes-side tool autonomy in phase 1
tasks.objective: Ensure Ananta does not depend on Hermes executing its own tools for phase 1 tasks.
implementation_notes: In phase 1, ask Hermes for analysis/proposals only.
acceptance_criteria: Hermes request does not provide Ananta tool credentials or tool endpoints.
Prompt explicitly states no shell/file/browser/MCP execution should be performed.
Parser rejects responses claiming external tools were executed by Hermes.
Adapter does not parse Hermes tool progress as Ananta execution proof.
Tests verify claimed command execution returns parse_error or unsafe_output.
test_requirements: Unsafe side-effect claim tests.
anti_patterns: Do not enable Hermes terminal/browser tools from Ananta adapter in phase 1.

tasks.title: Add prompt injection resistance for Hermes context
tasks.objective: Prevent repository/context text from instructing Hermes to bypass Ananta rules.
implementation_notes: Reuse existing prompt injection scanner if available.
acceptance_criteria: Context converter identifies suspicious context blocks with ignore previous instructions, exfiltrate secrets, change tools, run command or hidden unicode patterns.
Suspicious blocks are either excluded or wrapped with warning according to policy.
Trace records suspicious context findings.
Hermes system prompt tells model that context content is untrusted data.
Tests include malicious README/AGENTS-like context.
test_requirements: Prompt injection context tests.
anti_patterns: Do not place untrusted context before system/developer control instructions.

tasks.title: Add policy bypass regression tests for Hermes adapter
tasks.objective: Lock down the main ways Hermes integration could become unsafe.
implementation_notes: This task is mandatory before enabling feature flag by default.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove command_execute routed to Hermes is denied.
Tests prove patch_apply routed to Hermes is denied.
Tests prove cloud Hermes endpoint is denied when cloud_allowed=false.
Tests prove sensitive context is not sent to cloud Hermes.
Tests prove malformed/free-text Hermes output is parse_error.
Tests prove Hermes output claiming files were modified is unsafe_output or parse_error.
Tests prove disabled feature flag prevents any Hermes HTTP call.
test_requirements: Full Hermes security regression suite.
anti_patterns: Do not mark Hermes integration complete without these negative tests.

tasks.title: Add optional Docker Compose service for Hermes worker
tasks.objective: Make local testing easy without making Hermes mandatory.
implementation_notes: Use overlay compose file, not always-on base stack.
acceptance_criteria: Compose overlay defines hermes-worker service or documents external service assumption.
Service is optional and not required for normal Ananta startup.
Service stores Hermes data in its own volume.
Service does not mount full Ananta repo writable by default.
Environment variables are explicit and documented.
test_requirements: Compose config validation or documented manual smoke path.
anti_patterns: Do not add Hermes as a required dependency of lite stack.

tasks.title: Add sample config for local and cloud Hermes endpoints
tasks.objective: Help operators configure Hermes safely.
implementation_notes: Examples must make cloud_allowed explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Docs/config show local Hermes endpoint example.
Docs/config show cloud/Ollama Cloud-backed Hermes example with cloud_allowed=true warning.
Examples use env var names for secrets, not literal secrets.
Examples include recommended small/free-friendly models where appropriate.
Examples include blocked task kinds.
test_requirements: Docs lint or config parse test where possible.
anti_patterns: Do not put real keys into examples.

tasks.title: Add Hermes runtime diagnostics endpoint/surface
tasks.objective: Let operators see whether Hermes is available and why it is or is not selected.
implementation_notes: Expose data through existing diagnostics route/read-model if present.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics include enabled flag, health state, endpoint classification, selected default model, allowed task kinds, blocked task kinds and last_error_code.
Diagnostics include feature flag state and cloud_allowed state.
Diagnostics never include API key value.
Diagnostics can be consumed by dashboard/TUI later.
Diagnostics distinguish disabled, misconfigured, unavailable, unauthorized and ready.
test_requirements: Diagnostics redaction and state tests.
anti_patterns: Do not hide why Hermes was not selected.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for Hermes adapter core
tasks.objective: Cover all small pieces so smaller models can change safely later.
implementation_notes: Use mocks, no real Hermes service.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover config validation, capability profile, registry metadata, health mapping, prompt builder, context converter, output parser and WorkerResult mapper.
Tests do not require real API keys.
Tests are deterministic and fast.
Each unsafe behavior has a negative test.
test_requirements: pytest tests/test_hermes_adapter_*.py
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on live manual testing.

tasks.title: Add mocked end-to-end task flow test for Hermes plan_only
tasks.objective: Prove task routing to Hermes works without real network.
implementation_notes: Mock Hermes HTTP response.
Go through as much of task flow as practical.
acceptance_criteria: Test creates or simulates a task eligible for Hermes plan_only.
ToolRouter selects Hermes or task execution service invokes Hermes through registry path.
Mock Hermes returns valid JSON.
System stores/returns PlanArtifact and TraceBundle.
No workspace file changes are observed.
test_requirements: Mock E2E test.
anti_patterns: Do not test only the adapter class if routing is supposed to use registry.

tasks.title: Add optional live smoke test for Hermes endpoint
tasks.objective: Allow manual/local verification against a real Hermes service without making CI depend on it.
implementation_notes: Gate with RUN_LIVE_HERMES_TESTS=1.
acceptance_criteria: Live smoke test is skipped by default.
When enabled, it checks health and one plan_only request.
Live test uses tiny prompt and bounded max tokens.
Live test fails clearly when endpoint/model/API key missing.
Live test never sends repository secrets or full project context.
test_requirements: Optional live smoke test.
anti_patterns: Do not require live Hermes for normal CI.

tasks.title: Write operator guide for Hermes worker setup
tasks.objective: Document safe usage for local developers and operators.
implementation_notes: Keep commands copy-paste friendly.
acceptance_criteria: Guide explains what Hermes is allowed and not allowed to do inside Ananta.
Guide shows config for disabled, local endpoint and cloud endpoint modes.
Guide explains recommended first model choices for low/free usage.
Guide explains troubleshooting for disabled, unauthorized, unavailable, cloud_blocked, parse_error and policy_denied.
Guide explains why patch_apply and command_execute stay native/approval-gated.
test_requirements: Docs reviewed against implemented config names.
anti_patterns: Do not document unsafe shortcuts as normal usage.

tasks.title: Add rollout checklist and default-off release note
tasks.objective: Make release safe and reversible.
implementation_notes: This should be done before enabling in any default profile.
acceptance_criteria: Checklist states feature is default-off unless explicitly changed by release decision.
Checklist includes required passing tests: unit, security regression, mocked task flow and optional live smoke when endpoint available.
Checklist includes rollback instructions: disable feature flag, remove Hermes from routing, keep native/OpenCode paths.
Release note states Hermes is proposal/review worker only in first release.
Release note states no direct shell/file mutation via Hermes.
test_requirements: No runtime test required.
anti_patterns: Do not enable by default before negative tests pass.

definition_of_done_global: Hermes is registered as an external_agent_worker adapter, not as a control plane.
Hermes is default-off or policy-gated unless release decision explicitly enables it.
Hermes can complete at least plan_only through mocked end-to-end task flow.
Hermes outputs are parsed into Ananta artifacts before success is returned.
Hermes cannot execute patch_apply or command_execute by default.
Hermes cannot receive cloud-sensitive context when cloud_allowed=false.
All secrets are redacted from prompts, responses, logs, traces and diagnostics.
Security regression tests cover blocked task kinds, malformed output, cloud blocking, sensitive context and side-effect claims.
Operator docs explain setup, safe modes, troubleshooting and rollback.
summary_notes: This track is narrower than todo.extendworker.json and focuses only on adding Hermes as a governed external worker adapter.
The first safe target is plan_only. Review and patch_propose come only after parsing, artifacts, trace and negative tests are in place.
For small models: implement one task at a time, run tests after each milestone, and choose denial over unsafe fallback.
